message:
fix(search): reduce stopword list and increase tag weight for BM25

The extensive stopword list was filtering out terms that are valid
search keywords in programming contexts (e.g., "it", "do", "for", "while").

tokenize.ts changes:
- Reduce stopwords to minimal set: articles, conjunctions, and be-verbs
- Add comment explaining BM25's IDF naturally handles common words
- Preserve programming-relevant terms that were incorrectly filtered

bm25.ts changes:
- Increase tag weight from 1.5x to 2x (matching description weight)
- Tags are often the most specific signal for prompt relevance

These changes improve search recall for technical queries while
maintaining relevance through BM25's statistical weighting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2,16 +2,10 @@
 // Tokenizer and stopwords for BM25 search
 
 // Common stopwords to exclude from search
+// Kept minimal because BM25's IDF naturally handles common words,
+// and many "stopwords" (it, do, for, while) are keywords in programming.
 const STOPWORDS = new Set([
-  "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "but", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for",
-  "of", "with", "by", "from", "as", "is", "was", "are", "were", "been",
-  "be", "have", "has", "had", "do", "does", "did", "will", "would", "could",
-  "should", "may", "might", "must", "shall", "can", "need", "dare", "ought",
-  "used", "it", "its", "this", "that", "these", "those", "i", "you", "he",
-  "she", "we", "they", "what", "which", "who", "whom", "whose", "where",
-  "when", "why", "how", "all", "each", "every", "both", "few", "more",
-  "most", "other", "some", "such", "no", "nor", "not", "only", "own",
-  "same", "so", "than", "too", "very", "just", "also", "now", "here",
+  "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "but", "of", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been",
 ]);
 
 // Single-letter words to preserve (e.g., programming languages)
